feed.title: Euronews
feed.subtitle: Combined Euronews feed: News, My Europe, No Comment, Culture, Sport, Travel, and Next.

entry.title: France knocks Morocco out of World Cup in quarterfinal with same 2-0 scoreline as 2022
entry.content: For the second straight tournament, Morocco was ousted by the French 2-0 — this time in the quarterfinals on Thursday at Gillette Stadium, four years after losing by the same score in the semifinals.

entry.title: Burnham on course to become next UK PM with 322 Labour MPs backing him in first vote
entry.content: If Burnham reaches at least 323 nominations then it would no longer be mathematically possible for another challenger to get the 81 signatures required to join the race out of the total of 402 Labour MPs.

entry.title: South Florida's Palm Beach airport renamed President Donald J. Trump International
entry.content: Since returning to the White House, Trump has taken repeated measures to put his name and image in official spaces, an abrupt break with American political tradition.

entry.title: Is Belgium becoming a narco-state? Belgian politicians debate on The Ring
entry.content: Brussels and Antwerp have seen rising drug-related shootings and turf wars among trafficking networks. Has Belgium lost control of the drug issue? Brussels Mayor Philippe Close and conservative opposition politician Mathias Vanden Borre (N-VA) go head-to-head in this edition.

entry.title: 'Technical aspects' must be agreed for Patriots production in Ukraine, Zelenskyy says
entry.content: US President Donald Trump said Washington would give Kyiv "the right to make Patriots," the long range air defence missiles Kyiv has repeatedly requested to protect its cities and energy grid from Russian strikes.

entry.title: 'I'm not a candidate': Lagarde tells Euronews as French election speculation grows
entry.content: Lagarde told Euronews that she is not "a candidate", while vowing to continue championing European ideals. Speculation that she could leave the ECB early to play an active role in the campaign is rife in Brussels and Paris.

entry.title: 'Digital euro will not replace cash,' Lagarde tells Euronews as negotiations advance
entry.content: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde rejected claims in an exclusive interview with Euronews that the digital euro is designed to replace cash or allow the central bank to monitor payments, as the legislation entered a new phase following months of talks in the European Parliament.

entry.title: 'Let's debate it': ECB President tells Euronews as Spain pitches joint borrowing plan
entry.content: European Central Bank President tells Euronews in an exclusive interview that it is "pretty obvious we need to have a European asset" that the markets see as safe and liquid, and one that can compete with the US.

entry.title: OPCW reinstates Syria’s voting rights, citing 'significant change in circumstances'
entry.content: In 2013, Syria agreed to join the OPCW and disclose and hand over its toxic stockpile under Russian and US pressure and to avert the threat of air strikes by Washington and its allies.

entry.title: Portuguese rapper jailed in Germany for drug trafficking is deported to Portugal
entry.content: Ivo Vieira Silva, better known as rapper 18 Karat, was deported early on Wednesday. Sentenced in Dec 2022 to six years and three months, he will remain free in Portugal, but any return to Germany would mean immediate arrest to serve the rest of his term.

entry.title: Santas fill Danish streets for annual congress
entry.content: Dozens of Santas and Mrs. Clauses gather in Aalborg, Denmark, for the World Santa Claus Congress, with parades, family activities and summer festivities.

entry.title: News outlets seek sanctions against OpenAI in copyright battle
entry.content: OpenAI has been "hiding and destroying evidence" of how it trained ChatGPT on copyrighted news content, US media organisations allege as legal costs in the landmark copyright battle top $28 million.

entry.title: Europe must not ignore Egypt's persecuted Baha'is
entry.content: In a Euronews opinion piece, MEP Nikos Papandreou urges the EU to press Egypt to end discrimination against the Baha'i community. He argues Cairo should honour its constitutional commitment to freedom of belief by granting Baha'is equal legal rights.

entry.title: EU set to greenlight Hungary's €10 billion recovery envelope
entry.content: The decision would pave the way for the release of post-pandemic funds that had long been withheld over corruption concerns.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 9th, 2026 – Evening
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 9th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: Poland jails former Russian opposition activist and his wife for spying for Moscow
entry.content: According to court documents, Russian citizen Igor R., identified by Russian media as Igor Rogov, was jailed for seven years while his wife Irina was sentenced to three years.

entry.title: Growing momentum for EU joint borrowing, Cuerpo tells Euronews
entry.content: Spanish Finance Minister Carlos Cuerpo used finance ministers' meetings in Brussels to promote his €850 billion proposal for joint EU borrowing, arguing that the timing is right as Europe seeks to develop new safe assets in response to growing geopolitical uncertainty.

entry.title: Newly released footage shows revolver gifted by Erdogan to NATO leaders
entry.content: Images shared by the Lithuanian Presidency show a revolver given by Turkey's Erdogan to every NATO leader as a parting gift after the summit in Ankara. Each leader received a box containing a revolver engraved with their name, six bullets and a note exempting the weapons from export control.

entry.title: AI for Good summit takes place in Geneva as countries debate global governance
entry.content: Launched in 2017, this year’s AI for Good summit has set a new visitor record.

entry.title: Mosque replica and anti-immigration signs appear on Northern Ireland bonfire
entry.content: A loyalist bonfire in Moygashel, Northern Ireland displays a mosque replica and anti-immigration signs ahead of Orange Order celebrations.

entry.title: Ukraine opens criminal probe after crowds attack army conscription vehicle in Lviv
entry.content: Violence against recruitment officers was almost non-existent at the start of the war, but has proliferated in recent years as the fighting has dragged on and fatigue set in among the population.

entry.title: EU to probe Chinese Pekin duck imports as market-flooding row hots up
entry.content: Trade negotiations launched last week between Brussels and Beijing could be disrupted by an EU investigation into Chinese Pekin duck producers, following a complaint by EU farmers that cheap imports of Chinese poultry are harming their industry.

entry.title: Art under attack: Ramallah Contemporary Arts Festival returns with revamped programme
entry.content: After a two-year hiatus enforced by Israel's war on Gaza and a broad halt to cultural life, the Ramallah Contemporary Arts Festival is back, resuming events for the first time since October 2023 with an expanded programme showcasing new art forms.

entry.title: At least 28 dead in fire at shoe factory in eastern China, state media reports
entry.content: Footage shared by state television CCTV showed firefighters dousing the facade and interior of a large, multi-story white building with fire hoses, as thick black smoke poured out.

entry.title: US approves sale of Tomahawk missiles to Germany, Chancellor Friedrich Merz says
entry.content: The United States committed to granting formal approval for the sale of Tomahawk missiles and ground-based Typhoon launchers by August, although the number of missiles acquired would remain classified.

entry.title: Firefighters tackle large Edinburgh blaze as historic building left in ruins
entry.content: Video footage circulating on social media shows huge orange flames and black billowing smoke rising above the building, which is on Princes Street in the heart of the city.

entry.title: European Parliament aims to exclude end-to-end chats from message-scanning regime
entry.content: A law allowing scanning of online communications to detect child sexual abuse material was amended by MEPs to protect users’ privacy. The new version could create a clash with member states.

entry.title: Mud and debris left behind after deadly floods in southern China
entry.content: Residents in Guangxi, southern China, clear mud and debris after deadly floods damaged homes and streets following heavy rainfall.

entry.title: Brussels eyes €200bn in savings by electrifying Europe's economy, draft reveals
entry.content: While earlier EU climate strategies focused primarily on cutting greenhouse gas emissions, the forthcoming electrification plan recasts the issue as one of economic competitiveness, highlighting its potential to reduce reliance on fossil fuel imports, according to a document seen by Euronews.

entry.title: Italy expels two Russian diplomats accused of spying, FM Antonio Tajani says
entry.content: The Russian foreign ministry quoted by state news agency RIA Novosti said on Thursday that Moscow would react with "an appropriate response."

entry.title: Paris City Hall draped in Les Bleus colours ahead of World Cup quarter-final
entry.content: Paris City Hall displays Les Bleus banners ahead of France's 2026 World Cup quarter-final against Morocco, showing support for the national team.

entry.title: Europe's strategic independence starts with energy
entry.content: In a Euronews opinion piece, the CEOs of ABB, Alfa Laval, Danfoss, Knauf Insulation, ROCKWOOL, Saint-Gobain, Schneider Electric, Signify, SSAB and VELUX Group argue that Europe's strategic independence depends on electrification, efficiency and clean energy.

entry.title: Erling Haaland’s 2016 rap song resurfaces and turns into viral World Cup hit
entry.content: Before he was spearheading Norway to their first-ever World Cup quarterfinal, MVP-contender Erling Haaland dabbled in rap. A song from 2016 has resurfaced and exploded online, racking up millions of views and leading fans to say: “This should be played at the stadium if Norway wins against England.”

entry.title: Trade ban on Israeli settlements is the latest test of EU unity
entry.content: The EU is weighing a trade ban on Israeli settlements, with Italy possibly tipping the balance. However, deep divisions among member states remain, alongside unresolved questions about the legal basis, and a formal proposal is still far away.

entry.title: Western Europe recorded hottest June as heatwaves surge, EU's climate monitor says
entry.content: Global temperatures in June were 1.39C above the estimated pre-industrial average, a period covering 1850-1900, according to Copernicus.

entry.title: Up to 38°C at the weekend: how long will this heatwave last in Germany?
entry.content: Several German cities face another weekend of tropical nights and daytime highs above 35C. However, weather services say the heatwave will not cover all of Germany.

entry.title: NATO summit ended 'on a more positive note than feared', former NATO security advisor says
entry.content: Jamie Shea, former NATO security adviser, says NATO 3.0 is going to be "much more a European show and less an American show" than it used to be. According to Shea, the US "is going to do a lot less in terms of holding up the collective defence than allies were used to during the Cold War".

entry.title: Who is Count Binface – the alien space warrior standing against Brexit champion Nigel Farage?
entry.content: In a week when the race to become Britain’s next prime minister is effectively a one-horse race, there seems to be more suspense in another contest pitting Brexit champion Nigel Farage against a comedian going viral online. Is this a joke too far for Brits and their famous sense of humour?

entry.title: Von der Leyen to donate gun gifted by Erdoğan to military museum
entry.content: Personalised pistols given out by the Turkish president at this week's NATO summit appear to be gifts that keep on giving.

entry.title: Robert Wun unveils 'Childsplay' couture collection in Paris
entry.content: Robert Wun presents his 'Childsplay' couture collection during Paris Haute Couture Week, blending dramatic designs with childhood-inspired creativity.

entry.title: This Tuscan hotel with its own vineyard and golf club was just named the best in Europe
entry.content: Originally founded in the 1100s, Castiglion del Bosco, a Rosewood Hotel has been crowned the best property on the continent by Travel + Leisure.

entry.title: Commission to tighten access to EU market as foreign interference concerns rise
entry.content: A draft document shows that the EU wants to allow the exclusion of foreign companies that pose security risks from its public procurement market, with particular worries about data transfers and weaponisation of the bloc's dependencies on tech and minerals.

entry.title: Have your say and nominate the 7 Contemporary Wonders of the World
entry.content: The World Travel & Tourism Council (WTTC) has launched a global campaign to identify the 7 Contemporary Wonders of the World – inviting people to nominate modern landmarks built since 1801.

entry.title: Russian drivers face petrol shortages after Ukrainian strikes on Russian energy infrastructure
entry.content: Drivers in Saint Petersburg are struggling to find petrol after weeks of intensified Ukrainian strikes on Russia's energy infrastructure disrupted fuel supplies, leaving some filling stations without fuel and others forced to close.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 9th, 2026 – Midday
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 9th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: 'Total Eclipse Of The Heart' singer Bonnie Tyler dies aged 75
entry.content: Legendary Welsh singer Bonnie Tyler has died at the age of 75. She achieved international fame in the 1980s, with the release of the hit single 'Total Eclipse Of The Heart'.

entry.title: EU should make 'individual assessments' before returning migrants, says UNHCR representative
entry.content: Jean-Nicolas Beuze, UNHCR representative in Brussels, believes it is key for the EU to ensure "nobody is returning to a possible situation of harm" when implementing the recently approved Pact on Migration and Asylum.

entry.title: World Cup: Morocco fans dream of revenge as France quarter-final looms
entry.content: Morocco fans gathered outside the French team's hotel in Boston ahead of the World Cup quarter-final, convinced their team can finally overcome France and continue its historic run.

entry.title: NATO summit: Europe signals it's ready to take control of its own defence
entry.content: This week's summit in Ankara was the first building block in the Europeanisation of NATO, with the continent apparently having decided that its future depends on weaning itself off US military support.

entry.title: Artemis II astronauts reunite with Orion after record-breaking Moon mission
entry.content: What comes next for NASA’s Artemis missions after a historic lunar fly-around?

entry.title: Brussels Airlines takes playful jab at Spain ahead of World Cup quarterfinal clash
entry.content: Brussels Airlines and Norwegian are taking the 2026 FIFA World Cup rivalries to the skies with tongue-in-cheek jabs at their European counterparts.

entry.title: “NATO works”: Estonia’s PM pushes back on alliance doubts
entry.content: “NATO is working well” — Speaking exclusively to Euronews’ Shona Murray as part of our special report live from Ankara, Estonia’s Prime Minister Kristen Michal shares his views on Trump, Russia, European defence and whether NATO is entering a new era.

entry.title: Exclusive: Brussels puts trade ban with Israeli settlements on the table
entry.content: The EU executive has laid out several options to further restrict EU imports of goods produced in Israeli settlements in the occupied Palestinian territories, after a majority of EU foreign ministers called for clarifying what restrictive trade measures could be taken during a meeting in June.

entry.title: World Cup sparks wave of racist misinformation online
entry.content: False claims and AI-generated videos with racist overtones have circulated during the FIFA World Cup alongside genuine incidents of racism and xenophobia.

entry.title: Russia bans diesel exports amid deepening fuel crisis
entry.content: "This will enable us to increase supplies to the domestic market in July," Russian Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak said.

entry.title: Dogs and cats at work: Yes or no? Here are Europe's most pet-friendly countries
entry.content: In a new survey of 16,000 workers, more than half would consider switching job for a dog-friendly company.

entry.title: Unvisited Sweden: New map reveals the nature spots far, far away from the crowds
entry.content: In a bid to ease pressure on popular destinations, a new project highlights 24 locations in Sweden where you’re incredibly unlikely to run into anybody else.

entry.title: Meta plans biggest AI data centre outside US in Canada with $9.1bn investment
entry.content: Meta will pour more than $9.1bn (€8.4bn) into a new artificial intelligence data centre in Alberta, Canada — its largest outside the United States — powered by a dedicated natural gas plant.

entry.title: “Sometimes not answering is the best answer”: Xavier Bettel on bullies
entry.content: In an exclusive interview as part of Euronews’ live special report from Ankara, Luxembourg’s Deputy Prime Minister and Minister for Foreign Affairs, Xavier Bettel, speaks with NATO correspondent Shona Murray about Europe’s position in an increasingly uncertain world.

entry.title: More than just a World Cup quarterfinal: France and Morocco gear up for knockout clash
entry.content: Les Bleus last faced the Atlas Lions in the 2022 World Cup semifinals, where France managed to edge Morocco, the tournament's surprise package, in a 2-0 win.

entry.title: Belgium's Defence Minister: "The Iranian regime is always lying"
entry.content: "You cannot build Rome in one day." Belgium's Defence Minister Theo Francken joins Euronews' Shona Murray for a special report live from the NATO Summit in Ankara, discussing Ukraine, Iran, NATO and Belgium's largest defence investment in more than two decades.

entry.title: 'Maldives of the Middle East': Saudi Arabia's Red Sea project takes shape
entry.content: Saudi Arabia is developing a luxury Red Sea destination of untouched islands, coral reefs, desert and mountains to entice the world’s most exclusive travellers.

entry.title: Newsletter: Another day, another diatribe
entry.content: In today's newsletter: Europeans leave the NATO summit with loaded guns plus strong statements from US President Donald Trump, EU ambassadors may go into overdrive over the 21st package of sanctions against Russia, and a European Parliament vote is set to rile up the political right.

entry.title: From sewers to swimming sites: how Europe's cities reclaim their rivers
entry.content: As Europe braces for hotter summers, cities are reopening rivers once written off as polluted waterways. From Paris to Copenhagen, local authorities are investing in cleaner, swimmable rivers to adapt to rising temperatures and meet citizens' needs.

entry.title: Andy Burnham left with clear path to Downing Street as last potential rival rules out leadership bid
entry.content: Former UK armed forces minister Al Carns said Burnham had his "full backing" and that he had "earned this".

entry.title: Justin Bieber joins Madonna, Shakira and BTS for FIFA World Cup first ever final halftime show
entry.content: Justin Bieber has joined a star-studded list of halftime performers who will close out the 2026 World Cup in New York.

entry.title: Wrestlers train for Mongolia's iconic Naadam festival
entry.content: Mongolian wrestlers train at Basu camp with sparring, fitness and football drills as they prepare for the annual Naadam festival.

entry.title: Europe Today: Trump overshadows NATO summit with renewed Iran strikes
entry.content: Renewed US strikes on Iran overshadow the close of the NATO summit, where allies pledged record defence spending and support for Ukraine. Meanwhile, the EU faces migration challenges, OLAF uncovers a counterfeit condom network and the World Cup reaches the quarter-final stage.

entry.title: Rio devotees celebrate Zé Pelintra Day with music, dance and prayer
entry.content: Dozens of devotees gather in Rio de Janeiro's Lapa neighbourhood to celebrate Ze Pelintra Day with prayer, music, dancing and candle offerings.

entry.title: Bolton: Trump talks about taking over Greenland to stir controversy
entry.content: Former US National Security Adviser John Bolton has dismissed Donald Trump's renewed calls for US control of Greenland as a deliberate attempt to provoke reactions, telling Euronews the US president often makes such remarks "because it drives people crazy".

entry.title: Trump's new Greenland threat triggers Europe's unhealed trauma
entry.content: The fight over Greenland’s future is far from over as Trump revives his bid to seize the island, reopening Europe’s deepest fears.

entry.title: 21st-century democracy: How Ireland consulted the public on its EU Presidency
entry.content: Ireland opened up its EU Presidency priorities to an online public consultation — and the results offer a blueprint for more transparent, inclusive governance across Europe.

entry.title: A new literary‑inspired Hilton hotel has opened in northern Iceland
entry.content: Located in the heart of Akureyri, Iceland’s ‘Capital of the North’, the new Skáld Akureyri, Curio Collection hotel features rooms and apartments inspired by Icelandic storytelling and Nordic design.

entry.title: Latest news bulletin | July 9th, 2026 – Morning
entry.content: Catch up with the most important stories from around Europe and beyond this July 9th, 2026 - latest news, breaking news, World, Business, Entertainment, Politics, Culture, Travel.

entry.title: June 2026 broke heat records across Europe and oceans, EU climate data reveals
entry.content: A rapid succession of major heatwaves since May suggests that extreme heat is no longer an isolated event, but an increasingly prolonged feature of European summers.

entry.title: Archaeology: 18 ancient tombs in Egypt yield 'golden tongue' amulets
entry.content: New excavations at Marina El Alamein, ancient Leukaspis, reveal 18 Ptolemaic and Roman tombs with rare amulets and gold sheets used in “golden tongue” rites, enriching Mediterranean archaeology.

entry.title: Iran launches retaliatory strikes on Gulf states as Trump warns US bombing could get 'much worse'
entry.content: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said they had struck US military bases in Bahrain and Kuwait in response to fresh American strikes. They also warned their responses would expand to other bases across the region if US attacks were repeated.

entry.title: March for peace in memory of the victims of the Srebrenica genocide
entry.content: Several thousand people gathered in Bosnia and Herzegovina on Wednesday to take part in a peace march in memory of the victims of the genocide and the survivors who fled the town of Srebrenica in July 1995.

entry.title: Trump skips new Air Force One on return from Turkey NATO summit
entry.content: Asked during a press conference whether the change was for security reasons related to Iran, Trump sidestepped the question by talking about previous alleged assassination attempts by Tehran.

entry.title: Tanks vs. tractors? Can the EU keep providing a political shield to farmers? MEPs debate in The Ring
entry.content: Tough talks on the EU’s next long-term budget, Ukraine’s push for EU accession and skyrocketing fertiliser costs are putting European farmers under the political spotlight once more. Can the EU continue to appease them as it shifts its focus to defence and global trade? MEPs debate on The Ring.

entry.title: Trump tempers fury with allies and ends 'tremendously successful' NATO summit on high note
entry.content: European leaders showcased their surging defence spending at the summit as they looked to convince Trump they're making good on a pledge to ramp up budgets and take more responsibility for their own security.

entry.title: 'Associating Islam with violence contradicts its true nature': Global Forum debates Islamic heritage
entry.content: More than 70 international projects were launched at Uzbekistan's first Islamic Civilisation Forum, where delegates called for greater scientific cooperation, education and preservation of Islamic heritage.

entry.title: Venezuela calls for release of frozen assets to help earthquake recovery efforts
entry.content: Meanwhile, the United Nations launched an urgent appeal on Wednesday to raise €259 million for relief operations.

entry.title: Barcelona sets new heat record of 40.5C, weather agencies say
entry.content: Scientists say human-driven climate change is increasing the intensity, length and frequency of extreme weather events such as heatwaves.

entry.title: Rescuers search for five missing people after deadly landslide in India's Kerala state
entry.content: Experts say human-caused climate change is intensifying South Asia’s monsoons, which traditionally run from June to September and again from October to December.

entry.title: Putting survivors at the heart of Europe's fight against child sexual abuse
entry.content: A new EU Directive ensures that victims of child rape can report the crime until the age of 50 – establishing a minimum standard across all 27 Member States, describes Jeroen Lenaers, MEP (Christian Democrats) in an OpEd for Euronews.

entry.title: French watchdog orders Meta back to press payment talks after copyright deals expire
entry.content: France’s competition authority warns Meta’s approach to copyright talks with news outlets could “seriously harm” the press

entry.title: Thousands embark on Bosnia peace march to remember Srebrenica victims
entry.content: Over 6,500 people took part in a peace march to remember the victims of the Srebrenica genocide and the survivors who walked for days to save their lives.

entry.title: Do World Cup hydration breaks protect players in the heat?
entry.content: Scientists are questioning whether FIFA's hydration breaks do enough to protect players competing in extreme heat. While some argue the breaks disrupt the game, researchers say longer cooling periods and active cooling measures may better reduce heat-related health risks.

entry.title: ‘People admire scars’: MEPs fear Le Pen more than Bardella
entry.content: The general feeling in the European Parliament is that the longstanding far-right leader has a better chance of becoming France’s president than her younger successor.

entry.title: Spanish PM Sánchez says ties with US 'very positive' after Trump threat to cut all trade
entry.content: Clashes with Trump and Israel have made Sánchez, one of the few remaining openly leftist voices in a Europe increasingly dominated by right-wing politics, a hero to many progressives around the world.

entry.title: AI's biggest World Cup star? It's a fake Erling Haaland
entry.content: Norwegian striker Erling Haaland is having the World Cup of his life — on the pitch and online. Here's why a lot of what you've seen of him is not real.

entry.title: Sixteen-year-old boy arrested after south Germany school attack, Bavarian police say
entry.content: Police said people should avoid the area following the incident in Schongau, a town just north of the Alps of some 12,000 inhabitants.

entry.title: 'We're gonna hit 'em hard tonight,' Trump says about further air strikes on Iran
entry.content: Trump had said that the ceasefire with Iran is "over" after Washington launched strikes on the country overnight, to which Iran responded by targeting US bases in the Gulf.

entry.title: Exclusive: EU to revise crypto rules in 2027 amid Trump's push for digital assets
entry.content: The European Union is set to revise its Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA) to cover non-EU issuers and broaden its scope, as US President Donald Trump's support for stablecoins prompts new regulatory questions.

entry.title: Family of Ruth Ellis, last woman executed in UK, wins posthumous government pardon
entry.content: A mother of two children, Ellis' execution in 1955 provoked an outcry and helped swing public opinion against the death penalty.

entry.title: US to give Ukraine licence to produce Patriot air-defence interceptors, Trump says
entry.content: Donald Trump said the US will grant Ukraine a licence to produce Patriot interceptors after more than six months of lobbying from Kyiv.

entry.title: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage's rivals rule out standing in Clacton by-election
entry.content: The shock announcement that he was stepping down came as Farage is the subject of a parliamentary probe over the non-disclosure of a €5.8 million donation from a Thailand-based crypto-currency billionaire.

entry.title: OpenAI, Meta and SpaceXAI push new AI models in a week of major releases
entry.content: It’s a week of major AI releases from some of the world’s biggest tech companies. Here’s what to expect.

entry.title: EU faces tougher gas storage refill task as winter looms and high prices bite
entry.content: EU gas storage was only 28 percent full at the start of the summer injection season, but energy regulators are confident in the system's resilience thanks to regasification capacity.

entry.title: Premier League clubs' pre-tax losses surged more than 600% in a single season
entry.content: English top-flight football clubs saw their combined pre-tax losses balloon from £135 million (€158mn) to £948 million (€1.1bn) in the 2024/25 season, even as their revenues climbed to a record high.

entry.title: EU narrows ban on Russian combatants to appease France and Italy
entry.content: A narrowed-down version of the ban on Russian combatants, limited to short-stay visas and those who fought directly, landed on the table of EU ambassadors meeting on Wednesday after France and Italy pushed back on the measure.

entry.title: Norway police says 28 people arrested in international child porn crackdown
entry.content: Norway's National Criminal Investigation Service (Kripos) said the men had used the cryptocurrency Monero to pay for access to several forums on the dark web.

entry.title: Eight member states oppose the opening of Serbia’s next EU accession cluster
entry.content: In a major blow to Belgrade’s bid to join the European Union, EU ambassadors who gathered in Brussels on Wednesday failed to find unanimity on Serbia's path toward joining the bloc.

entry.title: EU-backed project brings social housing back to Budapest
entry.content: A former school is being converted into Budapest's first new social housing in 25 years, with EU funding supporting a pilot project aimed at easing the city's worsening housing crisis.

entry.title: How Abu Dhabi is using museums, storytelling and innovation to redefine its global image
entry.content: Euronews Culture spoke to Steve Cook, the Executive Director of Strategic Marketing and Communications at the Department of Culture and Tourism for Abu Dhabi, about the emirate's ambitious cultural expansion plans, including the upcoming Guggenheim Abu Dhabi.

entry.title: Gulf states brace again for fiery US-Iran showdown as tensions spiral
entry.content: From the front lines of the US-Iran confrontation, the Gulf nations have again urged both sides, with strategic restraint, not to abandon their negotiations, while Tehran is telegraphing that the Strait of Hormuz is now Iran's.

entry.title: European Parliament condemns Zelenskyy for naming military unit after UPA heroes
entry.content: A resolution on Ukraine’s accession to the EU defines the choice as an “unnecessary and unprovoked escalation” by the Ukrainian president, reigniting Warsaw–Kyiv tensions.

entry.title: ETIAS: After EES chaos, will Europe’s new €20 travel authorisation system be delayed?
entry.content: According to a report in the Financial Times, the EU is set to delay the launch of the new European Travel Information and Authorisation System (ETIAS) until 2027.

entry.title: UK and Norway leaders don national team shirts ahead of World Cup clash
entry.content: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer and his Norwegian counterpart, Jonas Gahr Støre, held a bilateral meeting ahead of the NATO Summit in Ankara wearing their national team shirts. Both leaders backed their teams ahead of Friday's World Cup quarter-final in Miami.

entry.title: This flight app is making airport connections less stressful
entry.content: Flighty’s new connection assistant breaks down every step of your journey and predicts how long each will take.

entry.title: Apple loses challenge over EU app store rules
entry.content: Apple brought the action in an attempt to overturn the EU designating it a "gatekeeper", which requires it to open its App Store and iPhone operating system to greater competition from rival apps and services.

entry.title: 43 ancient figurines discovered in Peru reveal secrets of the Americas’ oldest civilisation
entry.content: Buried for nearly 4,000 years, 43 ancient figurines have been unearthed in Peru, revealing a rare glimpse into the rituals of the Caral civilisation - the oldest known civilisation in the Americas.

entry.title: Trump attacks Spain calling it a 'wasted cause' and orders trade to stop
entry.content: The US president had already threatened in March to take economic reprisals against Spain after Moncloa blocked the use of the Rota and Morón bases for Washington’s bombing campaign against Iran.

entry.title: Fake videos exploit Germany's East-West divide before key elections
entry.content: Researchers say a coordinated influence campaign they suspect to be linked to Moscow is impersonating German media to exploit East-West divisions ahead of Germany's September state elections.

entry.title: At least seven killed in fresh Russian strikes on Ukraine as NATO leaders meet in Turkey
entry.content: Russia has hit the Ukrainian capital with several large deadly attacks over the last week, killing more than 50 this month in a spate of ballistic missile and drone strikes.

entry.title: Spain pitches €850bn per year in common EU borrowing
entry.content: Spanish Economy Minister Carlos Cuerpo will pitch a new common debt mechanism ahead of a meeting of euro-area finance ministers in Brussels on Thursday.

entry.title: 'The Trump Curse': Is Donald Trump a bad omen for sports teams?
entry.content: Following the elimination of the US at the World Cup, social media went into a frenzy with claims of a “Trump curse”. There seems to be prior form, as fans are citing other losses linked to Trump’s team endorsements or even his presence at games.

entry.title: Got €200,000 to spare? This ultra-exclusive trip for the 2026 solar eclipse could be yours
entry.content: Meliá Hotels International’s travel package to the Balearic Islands for August’s total solar eclipse is so exclusive that it's only available for one booking at each of its two hotels. The package includes flights on a private jet, a stay in a presidential suite, and a viewing experience at sea.

entry.title: Erdogan gives world leaders a ceremonial welcome ahead of NATO summit
entry.content: NATO leaders including Trump and Zelenskyy arrive in Ankara for a state dinner hosted by Erdogan ahead of key summit talks in Turkey.

entry.title: More than 30,000 votes cast so far in search of South Korea’s top travel spots
entry.content: South Korea has extended voting for its ambitious travel directory of the 10,000 must-visit destinations across the country. So far, more than 30,000 people have already had their say.

entry.title: Who in Europe is the most affected by extreme heatwaves?
entry.content: Europeans are experiencing another record-breaking heatwave, but not everyone is affected equally. Who is the most affected by these extreme climate events on the old continent?

entry.title: Florence, Prague and Salzburg: These are the best cities in Europe, according to Travel + Leisure
entry.content: After votes were cast by more than 200,000 people, San Miguel de Allende in Mexico came out on top on the overall world list, with the highest ranked European city coming out 13th.

entry.title: NATO leaders to meet after Trump restates Greenland claim
entry.content: Trump dashed hopes of a two-day summit by telling reporters the US should "control" Greenland. The Danish prime minister hit back, calling on the US to "respect Danish sovereignty".

entry.title: 'Russian threat' moves to the Arctic, Canadian Foreign Minister warns
entry.content: "The Russian threat is moving further and further north towards the Arctic Circle. We need to be prepared at all times across various domains", Canada's Foreign Affairs Minister, Anita Anand, said in an interview with Euronews, referring to the purchase of 12 submarines.

entry.title: 'Greenland is not for sale' Denmark's Frederiksen reminds Trump
entry.content: The renewed dispute comes months after Donald Trump revived his long-standing ambition for the United States to acquire Greenland, a proposal repeatedly rejected by Denmark and Greenland's leaders.

entry.title: Celebrating Greece and its ancient gods: Annual polytheism festival takes place on Mount Olympus
entry.content: Mount Olympus is a sight to behold each summer as torchlight processions, symbolic ceremonies and theatrical performances bring communities together in a shared celebration of ancient Greek mythology.

entry.title: Le Pen 'will demonstrate innocence' despite embezzlement conviction, MEP Leggeri claims
entry.content: Marine Le Pen ally Fabrice Leggeri claims the National Rally presidential candidate will prove her innocence despite an appeals court upholding her conviction for the misuse of millons in EU funds on Tuesday.

entry.title: Watch: Fake condoms, counterfeit weight-loss pens and the toy labels testing EU borders
entry.content: Anti-fraud investigators at OLAF just broke up an international smuggling group. Criminals tried to slip their illegal cargo past European border checks as children's toys to evade customs controls. But the boxes did not contain toys at all.

entry.title: Ali Khamenei's coffin passes through thousands of mourners in Iraq's holy city of Najaf
entry.content: Funeral procession for Ali Khamenei passes through Najaf as thousands of mourners gather, touching the funeral truck and honouring the late leader.

entry.title: Newsletter: Something is rotten in the state of Turkey
entry.content: In today's newsletter: US President Donald Trump revives his annexationist rhetoric of the arctic island of Greenland, ambassadors in Brussels bristle over what to do about Serbia, and a letter about FIFA is making waves in the European Parliament.

entry.title: Dolly Parton musical to open on Broadway in time for her 81st birthday
entry.content: Dolly: A True Original Musical is set to open in New York in December. Parton has described the show as “a dream come true”.

entry.title: Which European countries have the cleanest swimming waters and why?
entry.content: Every summer, millions of Europeans head to beaches, lakes, and rivers. New data shows most of this water is safe, but not everywhere. Where are Europe's cleanest waters? Watch the video.

entry.title: Vikings, Victorians and vultures: UK’s first live-action historical show park to open in July
entry.content: The new attraction, Kynren – The Storied Lands, tells stories from England’s history both real and fictional across shows and immersive experiences.

entry.title: Could your skincare contain microplastics? | Euronews Tech Talks
entry.content: The EU restricted the use of microplastics in cosmetics in 2023, but your skincare products might still contain them. Why?

entry.title: Europe Today: Le Pen to run for French president as Trump revives Greenland row
entry.content: Marine Le Pen has announced she will be the far-right National Rally’s French presidential candidate after an appeals court yesterday cleared the way for her to run next year. In Turkey, NATO leaders meet for their second day of talks after Trump has reignited confrontation over Greenland.

entry.title: 'Once the curtain is drawn, freedom is absolute': The magic and mystery of old photo booths
entry.content: Following the 100th anniversary of the first Photomatons, Euronews Culture speaks to the enthusiasts restoring and preserving them for an analogue-starved world.

entry.title: Why ‘alpine summer’ is one of this year's coolest European travel trends
entry.content: According to Google Trends data, UK search interest for ‘alpine summer’ is up 82% year-on-year – with travellers seeking out mountain holidays for their coolcations in Europe.

entry.title: Dinner diplomacy: Von der Leyen and Erdoğan’s working dinner comes with a side of tension
entry.content: European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen will have to chew over much more than just hors d'oeuvres Wednesday evening at a working dinner with Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdoğan. Observers expect her to weigh papering over disputes from both sides with the country's geostrategic value.

entry.title: Lithuania seeks China reset while maintaining hard line on Russia
entry.content: Lithuania's incoming government has pledged to maintain a hard line on Russia while moving to restore diplomatic ties with China.

entry.title: Argentina survives major scare from Egypt and heads into World Cup quarterfinals against Switzerland
entry.content: Trailing 2-0 against Egypt with 11 minutes of regular time to play on Tuesday, the defending champions rallied for an improbable 3-2 victory and a spot in the quarterfinals against Switzerland, which defeated Colombia in a penalty shootout.

entry.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran after ships attacked in Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: NATO described the US attack as "necessary", as Iran warned Washington it would review what response was needed.

entry.title: Nairobi man shares home with rescued birds
entry.content: Nairobi's 'Birdman' Rodgers Magutha rescues injured birds and lives with them, while defending his unusual bond with wildlife and conservation work.

entry.title: Estonia and Ukraine sign drone pact to deepen defence industry ties
entry.content: 'Ukraine has developed one of the most competitive defence industries in the world' through its experience fighting Russia's full-scale invasion, Michal said.

entry.title: Argentina fans sing and dance after dramatic World Cup comeback against Egypt
entry.content: Argentina fans erupted in celebration after the defending champions came from two goals down to beat Egypt 3-2 and reach the World Cup quarter-finals.

entry.title: Argentina mount epic comeback to beat Egypt as they move into quarterfinals
entry.content: Argentina trailed 2-0 with around 11 minutes to play. A late Enzo Fernández header saw them snatch a 3-2 victory and move into the quarterfinals.

entry.title: Marine Le Pen vows to run in France’s 2027 presidential election despite fraud conviction
entry.content: The leader of the National Rally announced on French TV that she would remain a candidate for the 2027 presidential election despite a court finding her guilty of embezzling EU funds.

entry.title: Ratify Mercosur or risk losing South America to China, Uruguay warns EU
entry.content: In an exclusive interview with Euronews, Montevideo's foreign minister, Mario Lubetkin, talks about the Mercosur trade agreement, Uruguay's political options and Trump's attempts at imposing his will in the Western Hemisphere.

entry.title: Prince Harry loses London High Court privacy case against Associated Newspapers
entry.content: In a statement, an Associated Newspapers spokesperson called it an "overwhelming victory for the Daily Mail and its journalists" and a "magnificent vindication of the paper's journalism.

entry.title: Tibetan activists detained outside Chinese Embassy in New Delhi
entry.content: Tibetan Youth Congress activists are detained by police during a 'Free Tibet' protest outside the Chinese Embassy in New Delhi on 7 July 2026.

entry.title: International Olympic Committee eases restrictions on Russian athletes
entry.content: Russian athletes looking to return to international competition will now be able to compete as long as they "meet relevant anti-doping requirements," the IOC said.

entry.title: European Parliament triggers procedure to ban Alternative for Germany’s EU party
entry.content: Europe of Sovereign Nations will now face a process that could see it stripped of its status as a European political party, losing funding in the process. Far-right MEPs from other groups opposed the decision.

entry.title: 'Public media cannot lie': Hungary pulls plug on state broadcaster in post-Orbán purge
entry.content: Hungary's public broadcaster went off-air on Tuesday, replaced by an on-screen apology, after Péter Magyar's new government installed interim management and sacked staff accused of pro-Orbán bias.

entry.title: Lockheed Martin and Rheinmetall plan first ATACMS missile production in Europe
entry.content: German defence giant Rheinmetall is to produce missiles designed by US aerospace firm Lockheed Martin, the two firms announced on Tuesday, marking the first time the weapons will be produced in Europe.

entry.title: EU still can't prove €43 billion in home renovation funds actually saved energy
entry.content: The EU accelerated home renovations after the pandemic, but according to its own auditors, it still lacks convincing evidence that the program delivered the energy savings and value for taxpayers that justified its massive investment.

entry.title: Third tanker hit in Strait of Hormuz in 24 hours, maritime agency says
entry.content: The UKMTO maritime agency is investigating the incidents and advised vessels in the area to proceed with caution.

entry.title: Trump revives his call for US to 'control' Greenland
entry.content: Arriving at the NATO summit, the US president claimed Denmark hasn't sufficiently invested in the Arctic territory's security.

entry.title: Brussels pitches AI cybersecurity plan amid dependence on US models
entry.content: EU unveils an AI cybersecurity action plan, but offers mostly recommendations as Brussels leans on negotiated access to US AI models like Anthropic's Mythos, exposing its tech dependency.

entry.title: Why Europe struggles to turn world-class science into business
entry.content: In the global race for innovation, the winners are those who can finance, test, manufacture and scale up new technologies the fastest, writes Italy's former Prime Minister, Enrico Letta, in an opinion piece for Euronews, calling for a "fifth freedom" for the EU.

entry.title: Nigel Farage says he's resigning as MP and will run in 'people vs. the establishment' by-election
entry.content: "I've decided that the people of Clacton should be the judges of my actions," Farage said. "It's a chance to stick two fingers up to the entire establishment".

entry.title: What is GlobalEye, the Swedish aircraft set to become NATO’s new ‘eyes in the sky’?
entry.content: As NATO members discuss future defence capabilities in Ankara, Swedish defence company Saab announced its GlobalEye aircraft will become the alliance’s new “eyes in the sky”.

entry.title: Canary Islands' new voluntary tourist tax will fund local sustainability projects
entry.content: Rather than introducing a mandatory tourist tax, the Canary Islands are enlisting travellers to contribute to local projects aimed at restoring nature and supporting communities to make tourism sustainable.

entry.title: Zelenskyy renews call for Ukraine's NATO membership citing military might ahead of summit
entry.content: The Ukrainian President has renewed his push to join NATO, insisting his country has already proved itself a reliable partner and that its membership in the alliance would be “entirely natural”.

entry.title: NATO must become more European to reduce reliance on US security, von der Leyen and Rutte say
entry.content: "To stay transatlantic, we have to become more European," Mark Rutte said ahead of a NATO summit in Ankara, where European allies are expected to showcase their beefed-up defence spending plans.

entry.title: Goats roam free in this French Alpine village
entry.content: Les Lindarets in the French Alps is known for goats roaming freely through its streets, attracting visitors who feed and interact with the animals.

entry.title: Euronews launches Kazakh language broadcasting in Astana
entry.content: As part of its continued development in Central Asia, Euronews now takes the strategic step to launch its new Kazakh language service and bring its European-perspective journalism and global coverage to Kazakhstan.

entry.title: ‘Misaligned’: Controversial AI-generated 'actress' Tilly Norwood to make feature film debut
entry.content: The divisive digital “performer” is about to star in the upcoming Particle6 project titled 'Misaligned'. Get ready to roll your eyes when you read the plot synopsis.

entry.title: No passport needed: Use your face for a ‘fast pass’ in Qatar’s international airport
entry.content: Hamad International Airport and Qatar Airways have launched a new biometric service that uses facial recognition for passengers to have a paperless departure in Doha.

entry.title: French court upholds Le Pen's fraud conviction but clears way for 2027 presidential bid
entry.content: During the appeal trial, Le Pen denied that the National Rally had a system to embezzle European Parliament funds and said the party acted in "good faith."

entry.title: Ukrainian agent confesses to killing woman suspected of Monaco bombing, security service says
entry.content: The announcement comes after authorities across Europe were hunting for the woman suspected of planting a bomb in Monaco that wounded three people, including a Ukrainian-born tycoon and his teenaged son.

entry.title: Hyperion GR-1 enters orbit: A new step for Greece's space programme
entry.content: It is the first Greek optical microsatellite developed under the National Microsatellite Programme - launched from Cape Canaveral.

entry.title: Parkout, the app connecting drivers to parking spaces in Athens
entry.content: It operates in Athens districts without controlled parking, outside the historic centre, and lets drivers signal a freed space so a nearby motorist can claim it.

entry.title: Is Google using your searches to train its AI? Here's how to opt out
entry.content: The changes reflect a broader industry shift. Rather than relying solely on data scraped from the web, companies are increasingly mining people's everyday interactions with digital services.

entry.title: European Parliament approves free cabin luggage and new delay compensation rules for air passengers
entry.content: Lawmakers gave the final green light to the revised regulation after a decade of talks with EU countries. Airlines argue the new rules will inevitably lead to higher ticket prices. Passenger associations say rights have been upheld.

entry.title: 'Portugal is an energy island and needs more flexibility,' minister says
entry.content: The Portuguese government argues that because limited connections isolate Iberia from the European grid, the country faces many of the same challenges as the EU's outermost regions and deserves compensation for the lack of interconnections.

entry.title: European Parliament to ask for probe into FIFA boss Infantino over Balogun red card
entry.content: A letter circulating in the European Parliament calls for an investigation into whether pressure from the US administration influenced FIFA's decision to reverse American striker Folarin Balogun's red card suspension. Belgium went on to rout Team USA 4-1 in a resounding victory.

entry.title: US President Donald Trump arrives in Turkey to attend NATO summit
entry.content: The two-day gathering in Ankara comes a year after NATO members pledged to ramp up security-related spending to 5% of GDP under pressure from the US leader. Yet, the US President has lashed out at European allies over Iran.

entry.title: Dustin Hoffman and Maggie Gyllenhaal honoured at Karlovy Vary Film Festival opening ceremony
entry.content: The 60th Karlovy Vary International Film Festival opened with a star-studded ceremony, welcoming Hollywood names including Dustin Hoffman, Maggie Gyllenhaal, Harvey Keitel and Jesse Eisenberg.

entry.title: South Korean law targeting 'fake news' takes effect as journalists' groups raise concerns
entry.content: South Korea saw a surge in online misinformation following the botched 2024 martial law attempt, including unsubstantiated allegations of Chinese interference in the electoral system.

entry.title: Has online shopping from outside the EU become more expensive?
entry.content: Several social media users are claiming that online shopping has become more expensive because of a new EU import charge. The Cube takes a look.

entry.title: ‘Overturn this’: Belgium mocks Donald Trump and FIFA with dance and post after victory against US
entry.content: Belgium has ended the USA’s World Cup campaign, with many taking great joy in the host nation’s humiliating 4-1 defeat following the controversy over FIFA’s decision to suspend a US red card prior to the game.

entry.title: Euro area 'under strain' as recession risks loom
entry.content: Rolf R. Strauch, Chief Economist at the European Stability Mechanism, said governments "have to create growth" to avoid a possible recession. The global instability poses "high demands" in governments that have to face them with limited fiscal space.

entry.title: Macron tours Damascus' Umayyad Mosque during Syria visit
entry.content: French President Emmanuel Macron visits Damascus' Umayyad Mosque with Syrian leader Ahmed al-Sharaa and signs the guestbook during his Syria trip.

entry.title: Nothing beats a Jet2 holiday, but what about a Jet2 wedding?
entry.content: Planning a wedding in Europe can be a lot to organise, but now Jet2 has added new destinations for the big day, including Malta, Italy, Greece, Spain, Türkiye and Portugal.

entry.title: NATO to put pressure on allies not stepping up, warns Dutch minister
entry.content: As the Netherlands announced an extra €3 billion euros new spending at the NATO summit in Ankara, Dutch Defence Minister Dilan Yeşilgöz-Zegerius tells Europe Today that extra pressure will be applied to NATO allies not stepping up on defence.

entry.title: Could military-age Ukrainian men lose temporary protection status in Germany?
entry.content: More than 260,000 Ukrainians of fighting age are living in Germany. As Kyiv and Berlin discuss ways to encourage their return, the question is: would they go back?

entry.title: US fans react as Belgium ends World Cup dream
entry.content: Belgium ended the United States' World Cup campaign with a 4-1 victory in Seattle, booking a place in the quarter-finals.

entry.title: Love, power and betrayal: Tosca rehearsals begin at Austria's St Margarethen quarry
entry.content: Rehearsals for Tosca at Austria's St Margarethen Quarry are underway, opening an evocative journey into Puccini's Rome, where light and shadow meet faith and revolution.

entry.title: Argentina fans gather in Atlanta for traditional World Cup 'banderazo'
entry.content: Hundreds of Argentina supporters gathered in Atlanta for a traditional banderazo rally ahead of their World Cup last-16 match against Egypt.

entry.title: Cooling down a 'logistical nightmare' at Tour de France, champ Tadej Pogacar says
entry.content: Beyond just the heat, Monday's third stage from Granollers in Spain, over the border into Les Angles in France, was threatened by wildfires which had broken out around 70km from the finish.

entry.title: How Europe made most, but not all, of its swimming waters 'excellent'
entry.content: Planning a swim this summer? Chances are you'll be diving into clean water. A new European Environment Agency report shows that 96 percent of monitored bathing sites across the EU meet minimum health standards, with nearly 85 percent rated "excellent". But why are some swimming spots still unsafe?

entry.title: San Fermín festival begins with Pamplona's famous bull run
entry.content: The first San Fermin 2026 bull run takes place in Pamplona as runners race six bulls through the streets during the famous 'encierro'.

entry.title: At least 18 wounded in Damascus blasts during Macron visit, Syrian officials say
entry.content: Macron had entered the presidential palace to meet Syrian President Ahmad al-Sharaa when the explosions happened near the Four Seasons Hotel.

entry.title: When do you need to hand in a sick note in Europe and which sick pay systems are the most generous?
entry.content: As Germany moves to clamp down on what the government sees as too many people taking sick leave, how do sick leave pay and medical certificate rules compare across Europe?

entry.title: Party Rock Anthem singer Lauren Bennett dies aged 37
entry.content: The British singer Lauren Bennett, who sang on the 2011 electronic dance hit 'Party Rock Anthem' has died at the age of 37.

entry.title: Hungry for a slice? Here are the best places in Europe to eat pizza... except Italy
entry.content: From London to Zagreb, the 2026 Top Pizza Europa awards reveal the best places to eat pizza. While Italy will always take the pizza crown, here’s what’s on the menu across the rest of the continent.

entry.title: Giant Lego World Cup trophy unveiled in New York
entry.content: A giant Lego FIFA World Cup trophy built from 1.36 million bricks is unveiled at Rockefeller Plaza in New York by Cafu and designer Christophe Vietti.

entry.title: 'No lasting peace' in the Middle East without a Palestinian state, Egypt's al-Sisi says
entry.content: The Egyptian president renewed Cairo's call for an independent Palestinian state during the inauguration of the country's new Strategic Command Headquarters, known as the Octagon.

entry.title: Watch: Planning to visit Europe this summer? The new Entry/Exit System means longer queues
entry.content: If you hold a non-EU passport, Brussels has a brand-new welcome package for your next European holiday. The Entry/Exit System was designed to log your face and fingerprints in around 70 seconds to catch overstayers. But the system isn't working as intended.

entry.title: Newsletter: NATO braces for impact
entry.content: In this newsletter: 32 North Atlantic Treaty Organisation (NATO) members meet for an annual summit in the Turkish capital aiming to keep Trump onside, and a French appeals court will offer a decisive ruling for presidential hopeful Marine Le Pen.
